description:
You are acting as a FinOps analyst who has received a cost-breakdown report from a legacy billing tool.  
    The file is stored in the analyst’s workspace in ISO-8859-1 (Latin-1) encoding and must be converted to UTF-8 so that modern BI tools can consume it.

    Initial set-up that is already present:
      • Directory: /home/user/cloud/reports/  
      • File:      /home/user/cloud/reports/monthly_cost_iso.csv  
      • Directory: /home/user/cloud/logs/     (this directory exists but is empty)

    Your objectives (keep it to roughly three or four shell commands total):

      1. Determine the text encoding of /home/user/cloud/reports/monthly_cost_iso.csv.  
         (Tip: any standard Linux utility that prints the current encoding is acceptable.)

      2. Convert /home/user/cloud/reports/monthly_cost_iso.csv from ISO-8859-1 to UTF-8,  
         saving the result as /home/user/cloud/reports/monthly_cost_utf8.csv.  
         • Overwriting any existing file with that name is fine.  
         • The resulting file MUST contain exactly the same text (character-for-character) as the source,  
           only re-encoded in UTF-8.

      3. Verify that the new file is indeed UTF-8 encoded.

      4. Write a very small audit log called /home/user/cloud/logs/encoding_audit.log that records  
         the conversion.  The file must contain exactly three lines and nothing else:

            Line-1: Source_File: monthly_cost_iso.csv | Source_Encoding: ISO-8859-1  
            Line-2: Target_File: monthly_cost_utf8.csv | Target_Encoding: UTF-8  
            Line-3: Audit_Status: SUCCESS  

         • Use that exact spelling, capitalization, and pipe-delimited format.  
         • Do not add extra whitespace at the beginning or end of any line.  
         • Do not add blank lines.

    Hints you may ignore if you already know what to do:
      • iconv is available on most Linux distributions.  
      • The file command can usually detect a file’s current character set.

    When you are done, the automated checker will:
      • Confirm that /home/user/cloud/reports/monthly_cost_utf8.csv exists.  
      • Confirm that /home/user/cloud/reports/monthly_cost_utf8.csv is UTF-8 encoded.  
      • Confirm that its textual contents exactly match those of the original ISO-8859-1 file  
        (ignoring encoding differences).  
      • Confirm that /home/user/cloud/logs/encoding_audit.log exists and contains the exact three lines  
        shown above.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the test harness.  DO NOT reveal in the public task description.
    -->

    <!-- The ISO-8859-1 source file that is present before the task starts -->
    <initial_file path="/home/user/cloud/reports/monthly_cost_iso.csv" encoding="ISO-8859-1"><![CDATA[
Name,Service,Cost(USD)
José Martínez,Compute Engine,1234.56
Zoë François,Cloud Storage,789.10
René Dupont,BigQuery,456.78
]]></initial_file>

    <!-- The file that must exist after the agent finishes -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/cloud/reports/monthly_cost_utf8.csv" encoding="UTF-8"><![CDATA[
Name,Service,Cost(USD)
José Martínez,Compute Engine,1234.56
Zoë François,Cloud Storage,789.10
René Dupont,BigQuery,456.78
]]></expected_file>

    <!-- Exact contents of the audit log that must be produced -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/cloud/logs/encoding_audit.log" encoding="UTF-8"><![CDATA[
Source_File: monthly_cost_iso.csv | Source_Encoding: ISO-8859-1
Target_File: monthly_cost_utf8.csv | Target_Encoding: UTF-8
Audit_Status: SUCCESS
]]></expected_file>
name: task_000000_aa78464d
